architecture_gap: An endlessly rotating merge is not enough; the AAS needs a freeze-capable target architecture once convergence is evident.
data_model_changes: Add pressure-test traces, adversarial traces, convergence metrics, and finalization metadata to canonical state.
governance_implications: Councils can now terminate exploration deliberately once two convergence conditions are satisfied.
invention_concept: Merge the strongest stabilizing, registry, and recursive-evolution modules into one canonical runtime architecture.
migration_strategy: Apply the merged architecture in the next CSSM version and freeze if convergence is achieved.
problem_statement: A bounded evolution cycle requires one strongest architecture selected from Alpha, Beta, and Gamma after review under pressure and adversarial conditions.
proposed_architecture_change: Promote the reviewed merged architecture into the next CSSM version and freeze it once convergence conditions are satisfied.
protocol_implications: Architecture selection becomes pressure-tested, adversarial-reviewed, and convergence-bounded.
security_implications: The selected architecture includes replay safety, recovery control, telemetry durability, and governance freeze control.
title: Cycle 101 Converged Meta-Architecture
